mappings.rationaleAI: Covers the broader context of African history including the Nilotic Sudan and Ethiopia discussed in this section.
Relevant to subsections A2-A3 on the spread of Muslim domination in the Nilotic Sudan from c. 639 and the Mamluk attacks.
Relevant to subsections A2-A3 on the spread of Islam, the Funj, and the Islamic transformation of the Sudan.
Relevant to subsections A1 and B on the medieval Christian kingdoms of Nubia and the Coptic Christian tradition of Ethiopia.
Relevant to subsections A1, B1, and B2 on the Christian kingdoms of Nobatia and Maqurrah, and Ethiopian Christianity under the Solomonid dynasty.
Relevant to subsection A4 on the Egyptian occupation and the growth of British influence under Isma'il Pasha.
Relevant to subsection A2 on the Mamluk attacks on the Nilotic Sudan in the 13th and 14th centuries.
Relevant to subsection B on the interplay between indigenous African religions, Christianity, and Islam in Ethiopia.
Relevant to subsection A2 on the early spread of Muslim domination from c. 639 into the Nilotic Sudan.
Relevant to subsection B3 on the brief conciliation with the Roman Catholic Church in early 17th-century Ethiopia.
Relevant to subsection A3 on the rise of the Funj and the spread of Islam in the Sudan after c. 1500.
